---
title: "Fold Core Workbook"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    theme: cosmo
---

**Status:** Internal maintainer documentation. Not a user-facing article and
not a contract. Trace of the fold core and surrounding call graph, verified
against the code on **2026-05-30** after the v0.1.8.8 Batch 1 source split.

**How to use this:** This workbook explains the fold-core source layout,
function roles, data flow, replay invariants, and where run/sweep intentionally
diverge. It uses file/function anchors instead of line-number claims because
line numbers drift during implementation. If an anchor looks stale, search for
the named function first.

The load-bearing invariant remains: **events are the source of truth; every
view is deterministic replay over events.**

---

## 1. Source Layout

v0.1.8.8 splits the former monolithic `R/fold-core.R` into four internal files.
The split is mechanical and behavior-neutral.

| File | Concern | Functions |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `R/fold-engine.R` | The live per-pulse execution engine and telemetry finalization | `ledgr_execute_fold()`, `ledgr_finalize_fold_telemetry()` |
| `R/fold-event-buffer.R` | Event-buffer capacity helpers and opening-position event rows | `ledgr_event_buffer_initial_capacity()`, `ledgr_event_buffer_next_capacity()`, `ledgr_event_buffer_checked_capacity()`, `ledgr_opening_position_event_rows()` |
| `R/fold-reconstruction.R` | Event metadata access, equity/fills reconstruction, sweep memory summary | `ledgr_typed_event_metadata()`, `ledgr_event_meta_at()`, `ledgr_equity_from_events()`, `ledgr_fill_row_buffer()`, `ledgr_fill_row_buffer_grow()`, `ledgr_fill_row_buffer_add()`, `ledgr_fill_row_buffer_data_frame()`, `ledgr_fill_row_buffer_tibble()`, `ledgr_fills_from_events()`, `ledgr_assert_events_in_fold_order()`, `ledgr_sweep_summary_from_ordered_events()` |
| `R/fold-metrics.R` | Fold-local metric helpers used by sweep summary and parity helpers | `ledgr_bars_per_year_from_pulses()`, `ledgr_metrics_from_equity_fills()` |

The files are still package-internal implementation detail. No public API is
created by this split.

---

## 2. High-Level Flow

```{mermaid}
%%| echo: false
flowchart TB
  run["ledgr_run()"]
  sweep["ledgr_sweep()"]
  spec["execution list / future execution spec"]
  core["ledgr_execute_fold()"]
  durable["persistent output handler\nDuckDB ledger_events"]
  memory["memory output handler\nordered events"]
  run_replay["run-path reconstruction\nbacktest-runner.R"]
  sweep_replay["ledgr_sweep_summary_from_ordered_events()"]
  results["equity / fills / metrics"]

  run --> spec
  sweep --> spec
  spec --> core
  core --> durable
  core --> memory
  durable --> run_replay
  memory --> sweep_replay
  run_replay --> results
  sweep_replay --> results
```

`ledgr_execute_fold()` is the single fold engine shared by run and sweep. The
two entry points diverge at the output handler and post-fold reconstruction:

- committed runs use the persistent output handler and reconstruct durable
  views after reading `ledger_events`;
- sweeps use the memory output handler and reconstruct compact candidate
  summaries from ordered in-memory events.

The future v0.1.8.8 typed execution-spec work should reduce drift in how run and
sweep build the execution payload, but it must not add a second engine.

---

## 3. The Fold Engine

### `ledgr_execute_fold(execution, output_handler)`

Role:

- walks pulses in order;
- builds a strategy-visible pulse context;
- calls the strategy;
- validates full named numeric targets;
- converts target deltas to next-open fill proposals;
- resolves fill cost;
- emits events through the output handler;
- mutates in-memory cash/position state immediately after the event write;
- buffers strategy-state updates;
- records loop telemetry and checkpoint flushes.

The engine is intentionally event-first. When a fill is emitted, the event write
and state mutation stay adjacent so durable run replay and sweep memory replay
see the same ordered event stream.

### Per-pulse sequence

```{mermaid}
%%| echo: false
sequenceDiagram
  participant Loop as ledgr_execute_fold()
  participant Ctx as pulse context
  participant Strat as strategy_fn(ctx, params)
  participant Fill as fill model
  participant Out as output_handler
  participant State as cash/positions

  Loop->>Ctx: current bars/features/state
  Ctx->>Strat: no-lookahead context
  Strat-->>Loop: targets + optional state_update
  Loop->>Fill: target delta + next bar
  Fill-->>Loop: fill intent or no-fill
  Loop->>Out: write_fill_events(event_seq)
  Out-->>Loop: next_event_seq
  Loop->>State: apply cash/position delta
  Loop->>Out: flush/checkpoint if needed
```

No-lookahead boundary:

- the strategy sees current-pulse bars/features and current state;
- the fill model uses the next bar for next-open fills;
- final-bar target changes can warn and produce no fill because no following bar
  exists.

### `ledgr_finalize_fold_telemetry(...)`

Role:

- trims preallocated telemetry vectors to the number of recorded samples;
- stores session telemetry through the output handler;
- writes final telemetry status.

This helper is called by orchestration code after fold completion/failure. It
does not alter fold behavior.

---

## 4. Event Buffer And Opening Events

### Event-buffer capacity helpers

`ledgr_event_buffer_initial_capacity()`,
`ledgr_event_buffer_next_capacity()`, and
`ledgr_event_buffer_checked_capacity()` define shared grow-by-doubling capacity
rules used by durable and memory output handlers.

Invariants:

- buffer growth never exceeds the fold's maximum event capacity;
- overflow fails loudly with `ledgr_event_buffer_capacity_exceeded`;
- durable run and sweep memory handlers use the same capacity arithmetic.

### `ledgr_opening_position_event_rows(...)`

Role:

- converts opening positions into ordered `CASHFLOW` events with `meta_json`
  flags;
- validates finite cost basis;
- assigns event IDs and event sequence numbers before the fold starts.

This is a current implementation compromise: opening positions are accounting
state seeded through `CASHFLOW` plus metadata. The explicit `POSITION_SEED`
event type is deliberately deferred to a v0.2.x RFC, not part of v0.1.8.8.

---

## 5. Event Metadata

### `ledgr_typed_event_metadata(events, order_idx = NULL)`

Role:

- reads typed metadata attributes from in-memory sweep events when present;
- optionally reorders the metadata in lockstep with sorted events;
- returns `NULL` when attributes are absent or malformed.

This is the fast path for sweep memory events. Durable DB events do not carry R
attributes, so replay falls back to `meta_json`.

### `ledgr_event_meta_at(events, typed_meta, i)`

Role:

- returns typed metadata for row `i` when available;
- otherwise parses `events$meta_json[[i]]`.

All replay paths should use this accessor rather than open-coding the typed
metadata / JSON fallback.

---

## 6. Reconstruction Helpers

### `ledgr_equity_from_events(...)`

Role:

- test/reference reconstruction of an equity curve from an event table;
- sorts by `event_seq`;
- maps events onto pulse timestamps with `findInterval()`;
- reconstructs cash, positions, realized PnL, cost basis, and unrealized PnL.

This function is not the production run-path reconstructor. It is an internal
parity/reference helper used by tests and sweep parity checks. The production
run path still reconstructs durable views in `R/backtest-runner.R`; sweep uses
`ledgr_sweep_summary_from_ordered_events()`.

Replay invariant:

- event streams are step functions over pulse time;
- a pulse sees the most recent event at or before that timestamp;
- no future event can affect an earlier pulse.

```{mermaid}
%%| echo: false
flowchart LR
  events["ledger events"]
  sort["sort by event_seq"]
  map["findInterval()\nmap pulses to latest prior event"]
  cash["cash cumsum"]
  pos["position / lot state"]
  equity["equity curve"]
  fills["fills table"]

  events --> sort
  sort --> map
  map --> cash
  map --> pos
  cash --> equity
  pos --> equity
  sort --> fills
```

### Fill row buffer helpers

`ledgr_fill_row_buffer()`, `ledgr_fill_row_buffer_grow()`,
`ledgr_fill_row_buffer_add()`, `ledgr_fill_row_buffer_data_frame()`, and
`ledgr_fill_row_buffer_tibble()` implement primitive-column fill materialization.

Invariants:

- column order and classes match the public fills table;
- `ts_utc` stays POSIXct UTC across buffer growth;
- rows are appended in event order;
- close/open split rows keep `CLOSE` before `OPEN`.

### `ledgr_fills_from_events(events)`

Role:

- test/reference reconstruction of the public fills table from events;
- walks events in `event_seq` order;
- applies lot-state accounting;
- skips `CASHFLOW` events after updating lot state;
- splits close/open fills when one event both closes an old lot and opens a new
  one.

FIFO and split-row ordering are load-bearing. A future optimization must keep
the same row order and realized-PnL semantics.

### `ledgr_assert_events_in_fold_order(events)`

Role:

- validates that sweep memory events are already in strictly increasing
  `event_seq` order.

Sweep memory reconstruction relies on the fold-produced order. If this assertion
fails, the issue is upstream event collection, not replay.

---

## 7. Sweep Summary

### `ledgr_sweep_summary_from_ordered_events(...)`

Role:

- production sweep memory reconstructor;
- assumes ordered in-memory events;
- reconstructs equity, fills, metrics, and final equity without a DB round trip.

This function intentionally duplicates parts of the reference replay algorithm
because sweep avoids the persistent read-back cost. `test-sweep-parity.R` is the
guard that keeps the memory path equivalent to the durable/event-reference
views.

Key differences from `ledgr_equity_from_events()` + `ledgr_fills_from_events()`:

- it validates existing fold order rather than sorting;
- it computes equity and fills in one pass over events;
- it uses typed metadata attributes when present;
- it returns compact candidate summary components for sweep result assembly.

---

## 8. Metrics Helpers

### `ledgr_bars_per_year_from_pulses(pulses_posix)`

Role:

- infers a simple annualization frequency from pulse spacing;
- falls back to 252 when fewer than two pulses are available.

### `ledgr_metrics_from_equity_fills(...)`

Role:

- computes standard sweep/parity metrics from equity and fills;
- honors a supplied metric kernel when present;
- computes total return, annualized return, volatility, Sharpe ratio, max
  drawdown, trade count, win rate, average trade, and time in market.

The public run metrics path has its own user-facing wrappers in `R/backtest.R`.
This helper remains the sweep/reference metric kernel bridge.

```{mermaid}
%%| echo: false
flowchart LR
  equity["equity curve"]
  fills["fills table"]
  kernel["metric kernel\noptional"]
  fallback["standard metrics\nfallback"]
  summary["sweep / parity\nmetric summary"]

  equity --> kernel
  fills --> kernel
  equity --> fallback
  fills --> fallback
  kernel --> summary
  fallback --> summary
```

---

## 9. Current Intentional Divergences

| Surface | Run | Sweep | Guard |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Output handler | Persistent DuckDB writer | In-memory ordered events | Event-stream parity tests |
| Reconstruction | Durable read-back in `R/backtest-runner.R` | `ledgr_sweep_summary_from_ordered_events()` | `test-sweep-parity.R` |
| Candidate identity | Run ID / config / snapshot identity | Candidate row + reproduction key | Promotion/reproduction-key tests |
| Telemetry/materialization | Durable run artifacts | Compact summary unless promoted | Artifact policy tests |

These divergences are accepted for performance and artifact-policy reasons. They
are not license to add new execution semantics.

---

## 10. What This Workbook Does Not Bind

This workbook is explanatory. The authoritative contracts remain in
`inst/design/contracts.md`, the active v0.1.8.8 packet, and accepted RFC/ADR
artifacts.

Not authorized by this workbook:

- a second execution engine;
- public distributed execution APIs;
- explicit accounting event types;
- one production replay kernel;
- target-risk phased pulse;
- compiled fold core;
- changes to durable identity bytes.

Those items require their own ticket/RFC path.
